title: Shri Ganapati Atharvashirsha
intro: Read Shri Ganapati Atharvashirsha with Hindi meaning. Chant to seek the divine blessings of Lord Ganesha.
description:
Om Bhadram Karnnebhih Shrinuyaama Devaah Bhadram Pashyemaakshabhir Yajatraah |
Sthirair Angaistushtuva Sastanubhih Vyashema Devahitam Yadaayuh ||
Svasti Na Indro Vriddhashravaah Svasti Nah Pushaa Vishvavedaah |
Svasti Nastarksyo Arishtanemih Svasti No Brihaspatir Dadhatu ||
Om Shantih! Shantih!! Shantih!!!

1. Harih Om Namaste Ganapataye | Tvameva Pratyaksham Tattvamasi | Tvameva Kevalam Kartaasi | Tvameva Kevalam Dhartaasi | Tvameva Kevalam Hartaasi | Tvameva Sarvam Khalvidam Brahmaasi | Tvam Saakshaadaatmaasi Nityam ||
2. Ritam Vachmi | Satyam Vachmi ||
3. Ava Tvam Maam | Ava Vaktaaram | Ava Shrotaaram | Ava Daataaram | Ava Dhaataaram | Avaanuchaanamava Shishyam | Ava Pashchaattaat | Ava Purastaat | Avottaraattaat | Ava Dakshinaattaat | Ava Chordhvaattaat | Avaadharaattaat | Sarvato Maa Paahi Paahi Samantaat ||
4. Tvam Vaangmayastvam Chinmayah | Tvamaanandamayas Tvam Brahmamayah | Tvam Sachchidaanandaadvitiyo Asi | Tvam Pratyaksham Brahmaasi | Tvam Jnaanamayo Vijnaanamayo Asi ||
5. Sarvam Jagadidam Tvatto Jaayate | Sarvam Jagadidam Tvattastishthati | Sarvam Jagadidam Tvayi Layameshyati | Sarvam Jagadidam Tvayi Pratyeti | Tvam Bhumiraapo Analo Anilo Nabhah | Tvam Chatvaari Vaakpadaani ||
6. Tvam Gunatrayaatiitah | Tvam Avasthatrayaatiitah | Tvam Dehatrayaatiitah | Tvam Kaalatrayaatiitah | Tvam Mulaadharasthito Asi Nityam | Tvam Shaktitrayaatmakah | Tvaam Yogino Dhyaayanti Nityam | Tvam Brahma Tvam Vishnustvam Rudrastvamindrastvamagnistvam Vaayustvam Suryastvam Chandramaastvam Brahma Bhurbhuvah Svarom ||
7. Ganaadim Purvamuchchaarya Varnaadims Tadanantaram | Anusvaarah Paratarah | Ardhendulasitam | Taarena Riddham | Etattava Manusvarupam | Gakaarah Purvarupam | Akaaro Madhyamarupam | Anusvaaraschaantyarupam | Binduruttararupam Naadah Sandhaanam | Samhitaa Sandhih | Saishaa Ganeshavidyaa | Ganaka Rishih | Nichridgaayatri Chhandah | Shri Mahaaganapatir Devataa | Om Gam Ganapataye Namah ||
8. Ekadantaaya Vidmahe Vakratundaaya Dhiimahi | Tanno Dantih Prachodayaat ||
9. Ekadantam Chaturhastam Paashamankushadhaarinam | Radam Cha Varadam Hastairbibhraanam Mushakadhvajam ||
Raktam Lambodaram Shurpakarnakam Raktavaasasam | Raktagandhaanuliptaangam Raktapushpaih Supujitam ||
Bhaktaanukampinam Devam Jagatkaaranamachyutam | Aavirbhutam Cha Srishtyaadau Prakriteh Purushaatparam ||
Evam Dhyaayati Yo Nityam Sa Yogi Yoginaam Varah ||
10. Namo Vraatapataye Namo Ganapataye Namah Pramathapataye Namaste Astu Lambodaraaya Ekadantaaya Vighnavinaashine Shivasutaaya Shri Varadamurtaye Namah ||
11. Etadatharvashirsham Yo Adhiite | Sa Brahmabhuuyaaya Kalpate | Sa Sarvavighnairna Baadhyate | Sa Sarvatah Sukhamedhate | Sa Panchamahaapaapaat Pramuchyate | Saayamadhiiyaano Divasakritam Paapam Naashayati | Praataradhiiyaano Raatrikritam Paapam Naashayati | Saayam Praatah Prayunjaanah Paapo Apaapo Bhavati | Dharmaarthakaamamoksham Cha Vindati | Idamatharvashirsham Ashishyaaya Na Deyam | Yo Yadi Mohaad Daasyati | Sa Paapiyaan Bhavati | Sahasraavartanaadyam Yam Kaamam Adhiite | Tam Tam Anena Saadhayet ||
12. Anena Ganapatim Abhishinchati | Sa Vaagmii Bhavati | Chaturthyaamanashnan Japati | Sa Vidyaavaan Bhavati | Ityatharvanavaakyam | Brahmaadyaacharanam Vidyaanna Bibheti Kadaachaneti ||
13. Yo Durvaankurairyajati Sa Vaishravanopamo Bhavati | Yo Laajairyajati | Sa Yashovaan Bhavati | Sa Medhaavaan Bhavati | Yo Modakasahasrena Yajati Sa Vaanchhitaphalamaavaapnoti | Yah Saajya Samidbhiryajati | Sa Sarvam Labhate Sa Sarvam Labhate ||
14. Ashtau Braahmanaan Samyag Graahayitvaa Suryavarchasvii Bhavati | Suryagrahe Mahaanadyaam Pratimaasannidhau Vaa Japttvaa Siddhamantro Bhavati | Mahaavighnaat Pramuchyate | Mahaadoshaat Pramuchyate | Mahaapaapaat Pramuchyate | Mahaapratyavaayaat Pramuchyate | Sa Sarvavidbhavati Sa Sarvavidbhavati | Ya Evam Veda | Ityupanishat ||

Om Bhadram Karnnebhih Shrinuyaama Devaah Bhadram Pashyemaakshabhir Yajatraah |
Sthirair Angaistushtuva Sastanubhih Vyashema Devahitam Yadaayuh ||
Svasti Na Indro Vriddhashravaah Svasti Nah Pushaa Vishvavedaah |
Svasti Nastarksyo Arishtanemih Svasti No Brihaspatir Dadhatu ||
Om Shantih! Shantih!! Shantih!!!
Iti Shri Ganapatyatharvashirsham Samaaptam.

faq.q1: What is the Shri Ganapati Atharvashirsha?
faq.a1: The Ganapati Atharvashirsha is a sacred Vedic hymn dedicated to Lord Ganesha. It is considered an Upanishad (the Ganapati Upanishad) and belongs to the Atharva Veda. The name literally means "The head/essence of the Atharva Veda regarding Ganapati," representing the highest knowledge that provides spiritual stability.
faq.q2: What are the main benefits of reciting this hymn?
faq.a2:
Devotees believe that regular recitation of the Atharvashirsha offers several benefits:
Removal of Obstacles: It is famously known as a Vighnaharta (remover of obstacles) stotram, helping to clear hurdles in life.
Mental Clarity and Wisdom: It is highly regarded for improving concentration, intellect, and knowledge.
Spiritual Purification: The text mentions that it liberates the reciter from major sins and spiritual impurities.
Fulfillment of Desires: Many believe that sincere and consistent recitation helps in achieving one’s goals and aspirations.
faq.q3: When is the best time to recite it?
faq.a3:
Daily: It is most effective when recited daily, preferably in the early morning for mental clarity or in the evening to release the day's stress.
Special Days: Ganesh Chaturthi is the most significant time for this recitation. Additionally, reciting it on Wednesdays (the day of Lord Ganesha) is considered highly auspicious.
faq.q4: Are there any specific rules to follow while reciting?
faq.a4:
While there are no overly rigid rules, a few traditional guidelines help maintain the sanctity of the practice:
Cleanliness: It is customary to bathe and wear clean clothes before starting the prayer.
Focus: Recite with a clear, calm mind and sincere devotion. Accuracy in pronunciation is valued in Vedic traditions, but your intent and dedication are considered the most important factors.
Posture: Sitting on a clean mat (ideally Kush grass or a woollen seat) in a peaceful environment is recommended.
Surrender: Approach the prayer as an offering to Lord Ganesha rather than just a mechanical ritual.
faq.a5: Yes, the Ganapati Atharvashirsha is open to everyone. There is no discrimination based on caste, gender, or background. Lord Ganesha is often called the god of the masses, and he is believed to respond to the sincerity of any devotee’s heart. If you are new to the text, it is a great practice to listen to audio versions first to get comfortable with the pronunciation.
